channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19177] TCU: handle nodemaps for user subdirectories
item.description:
For ID-mapped users in a nodemap, the trash can should have a top-level directory that is the nodemap name, which holds the per-user subdirectories. This will separate the subdirectory trees as .lustre/Trash/MDTxxxx/nodemap for each tenant, to isolate quota usage,

The nodemap configuration should allow specifying the Trash Can UID/GID/PROJID separately for each tenant using per-nodemap trash_can_uid, trash_can_gid, and trash_can_projid parameters on the nodemap. By default, if the nodemap is using an ID offset range, these IDs could be the last ID in the nodemap (e.g. 199999) to minimize the risk of conflict with other IDs.

When creating the per-user subdirectory, the directory name created in the .../nodemap/directory should be based on the client/tenant UID (e.g. "1000/") so that the nodemap user sees the directory name they expect, while the inode->i_uid should be the filesystem/global UID (e.g. 101000) so that the UID is correct for permission checks when the nodemap client is accessing it.
item.summary: TCU: handle nodemaps for user subdirectories

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: these parameters and topmost Trash object should be put into a refcounted struct, so that they can be changed on a per-client/tenant basis and referenced from the nodemap/export instead of being global to the MDT.

Could you please elaborate on the need for this? Without nodemaps involved, to what values are set the Trash Can UID/GID/PROJID? Maybe there is an HLD where this can be discussed and specified?

Here is what I commented in the HLD about this:

This seems very complex and likely unnecessary. And probably nobody will understand what values to set for the trash_can_uid, trash_can_gid, and trash_can_projid parameters, if set correctly. In case the "nodemap" type of trash can is used, the UID/GID/PROJID to which files in the trash are assigned could simply be the mapping to file system id space of mdd..trash_can_uid, mdd..trash_can_gid, and mdd.*.trash_can_projid. Most likely those values would be within the client-side range (which is typically [ 0, 99999 ]), and squashed otherwise. And the mapped ids would be guaranteed to be in the tenant's file system id space, distinct from any other tenant's.

We just use nodemap name to classify the unrm files or directories moving into Trash Can.
We can set the mode of the directory to make it not accessible from non-root client users.

I guess it could be acceptable. In the MTFL context, root on the client ('local admin') is mapped. And in the general case, if a nodemap has admin=1, then root on the client will be root on file system side, but in this case I guess it should not be a problem if they can see the nodemap name via this special trash can dir.

Hi  Sebastien Buisson

The HLD wiki is: https://wiki.whamcloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=351437962

See Section 1.6 Per-Tenant Trash Can

 

For ID-mapped users in a nodemap, the trash can should have a top-level directory that is the nodemap name, which holds the per-user subdirectories. This will separate the subdirectory trees as .lustre/Trash/MDTxxxx/nodemap for each tenant, to isolate quota usage

This is not great to 'leak' the nodemap name to clients. At least the nodemap name should be hashed or equivalent.

We just use nodemap name to classify the unrm files or directories moving into Trash Can.

We can set the mode of the directory to make it not accessible from non-root client users.

should the UID 500 map to 10000?

You have defined the trusted property to 1 on your nodemap, so it trusts ids as expected.
https://doc.lustre.org/lustre_manual.xhtml#alteringproperties
Set it to 0 to have client ids mapped into the file system id space.

For ID-mapped users in a nodemap, the trash can should have a top-level directory that is the nodemap name, which holds the per-user subdirectories. This will separate the subdirectory trees as .lustre/Trash/MDTxxxx/nodemap for each tenant, to isolate quota usage

This is not great to 'leak' the nodemap name to clients. At least the nodemap name should be hashed or equivalent.

The nodemap configuration should allow specifying the Trash Can UID/GID/PROJID separately for each tenant using per-nodemap trash_can_uid, trash_can_gid, and trash_can_projid parameters on the nodemap. By default, if the nodemap is using an ID offset range, these IDs could be the last ID in the nodemap (e.g. 199999) to minimize the risk of conflict with other IDs.

Could you please elaborate on the need for this? Without nodemaps involved, to what values are set the Trash Can UID/GID/PROJID? Maybe there is an HLD where this can be discussed and specified?

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60298
Subject: LU-19177 tcu: striped dir access for Nodemap and UID Trash type
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 11850d91388998becc6900a3dd4f6cd87e8885bd

For

When creating the per-user subdirectory, the directory name created in the .../nodemap/directory should be based on the client/tenant UID (e.g. "1000/") so that the nodemap user sees the directory name they expect, while the inode->i_uid should be the filesystem/global UID (e.g. 101000) so that the UID is correct for permission checks when the nodemap client is accessing it.

 

I wrote a test script to understand node map with ID map:

 

test_117x() {
        local mdtdev=$(mdsdevname ${SINGLEMDS//mds/})
        local client_ip=$(host_nids_address $HOSTNAME $NETTYPE)
        local client_nid=$(h2nettype $client_ip)
        local dir=$DIR/$tdir
        local file=$dir/$tfile
        local nm="tnm"


        do_facet mgs $LCTL nodemap_add $nm
        do_facet mgs $LCTL nodemap_add_range --name $nm --range $client_nid
        do_facet mgs $LCTL nodemap_modify --name $nm --property admin --value 1
        do_facet mgs $LCTL nodemap_modify --name $nm \
                --property trusted --value 1
        do_facet mgs $LCTL nodemap_add_idmap --name $nm \
                --idtype uid --idmap 500-509:10000-10009 ||
                error "unable to add idmap range 500-509:10000-10009"
        do_facet mgs $LCTL nodemap_activate 1
        stack_trap "cleanup_117w $nm"
        wait_nm_sync active
        wait_nm_sync $nm trusted_nodemap
        do_facet mgs $LCTL get_param nodemap.$nm.idmap


        mkdir $dir || error "failed to mkdir $dir"
        chmod 777 $dir
        $RUNAS touch $file || error "failed to touch $file as $RUNAS_ID"
        tpath="$tdir/$tfile"
        cmd="$DEBUGFS -c -R 'stat ROOT/$tpath' $mdtdev"
        do_facet mds1 "$cmd"


        stat $file
}

The output is as follows:

 

 

== sanityn test 117x: Nodemap Testing ==================== 17:44:36 (1752745476)
On MGS 192.168.23.138, active = nodemap.active=1
waiting 10 secs for sync
On MGS 192.168.23.138, tnm.trusted_nodemap = nodemap.tnm.trusted_nodemap=1
waiting 10 secs for sync
nodemap.tnm.idmap=
[
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 500, fs_id: 10000 },
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 501, fs_id: 10001 },
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 502, fs_id: 10002 },
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 503, fs_id: 10003 },
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 504, fs_id: 10004 },
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 505, fs_id: 10005 },
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 506, fs_id: 10006 },
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 507, fs_id: 10007 },
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 508, fs_id: 10008 },
 { idtype: uid, client_id: 509, fs_id: 10009 }
]
running as uid/gid/euid/egid 500/500/500/500, groups: 500
 [touch] [/mnt/lustre/d117x.sanityn/f117x.sanityn]
debugfs 1.47.1-wc1 (28-May-2024)
Inode: 164   Type: regular    Mode:  0644   Flags: 0x0
Generation: 1689747575    Version: 0x00000001:0000000f
User:   500   Group:   500   Project:     0   Size: 0
File ACL: 0
Links: 1   Blockcount: 0
Fragment:  Address: 0    Number: 0    Size: 0
 ctime: 0x6878c618:00000000 -- Thu Jul 17 17:44:56 2025
 atime: 0x6878c618:00000000 -- Thu Jul 17 17:44:56 2025
 mtime: 0x6878c618:00000000 -- Thu Jul 17 17:44:56 2025
crtime: 0x6878c618:eb9bd788 -- Thu Jul 17 17:44:56 2025
Size of extra inode fields: 32
Extended attributes:
  lma: fid=[0x200000401:0x4:0x0] compat=0 incompat=0
  trusted.lov (56) = d0 0b d1 0b 01 00 00 00 04 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 01 04 00 00 02 00 00 00 00 00 40 00 01 00 00 00 00 04 00 80 02 00 00 00 02 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 01 00 00 00 
  user.job (9) = "touch.500"
  linkea: idx=0 parent=[0x200000401:0x3:0x0] name='f117x.sanityn'
  trusted.som (24) = 04 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 
BLOCKS:


  File: /mnt/lustre/d117x.sanityn/f117x.sanityn
  Size: 0         	Blocks: 0          IO Block: 4194304 regular empty file
Device: 2c54f966h/743766374d	Inode: 144115205272502276  Links: 1
Access: (0644/-rw-r--r--)  Uid: (  500/    test)   Gid: (  500/    test)
Access: 2025-07-17 17:44:56.000000000 +0800
Modify: 2025-07-17 17:44:56.000000000 +0800
Change: 2025-07-17 17:44:56.000000000 +0800
 Birth: 2025-07-17 17:44:56.000000000 +0800
On MGS 192.168.23.138, active = nodemap.active=0
waiting 10 secs for sync
PASS 117x (31s)

 

 

Both the UID on MDT inode and CLIENT obtained UID are both 500.

 

I am not understanding the role of id map: 500-509:10000-10009...

should the UID 500 map to 10000?

do_facet mgs $LCTL nodemap_add_idmap --name $nm --idtype uid --idmap 500-509:10000-10009 ||
error "unable to add idmap range 500-509:10000-10009"

How nodemap affect the uid? just intermediate checking the permission of IDs, does not change the ID storing on MDT on-disk inode?

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60215
Subject: LU-19177 tcu: add nodemap for user subdirectories in Trash Can
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 6d461a39cbd874126cbaa38fd062c6155a98de10
